rulings.Case Title: IN RE: SAN FRANCISCO HOUSING AUTHORITY
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Leshauna Jacksons To Appear At Deposition And Request For Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday July 14, 2015, Line 1, PETITIONER SAN FRANCISCO HOUSING AUTHORITY'S Motion To Compel Leshauna Jacksons To Appear At Deposition And Request For Sanctions. Pro Tem Judge Katherine Gallo, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: The motion is unopposed and is granted. Le'Shauna Jackson to appear for her deposition on or before August 14, 2015 at a date, time and location selected by Petitioner San Francisco Housing Authority. Notice of deposition to be pursuant to the Code of Civil Procedure. Petitioner's request for sanctions is granted. Le'Shauna Jackson to pay Petitioner $3,219.76 no later than 5:00 p.m. August 14, 2015. Check to be made payable to San Francisco Housing Authority and Cholakian & Associates. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to klgallo@discoveryreferee.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: DAVID RAWLINGS ET AL VS. TALK FREE, INC., A DELAWARE CORPORATION ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Protective Order Staying Discovery
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, July 14, 2015, Line 10, DEFENDANTS DOUG COLE, AN INDIVIDUAL, JOI SCHEELE, STAN TEETS AND TALK FREE, INC'S Motion For Protective Order Staying Discovery Transferred to the Discovery Calendar to be heard at 9:00 am on July 20, 2015 in room 301. This is a discovery motion that was incorrectly noticed for the 9:30 am Law and Motion calendar when the moving papers were filed in April 2015. Because the motion has not been argued in front of a Judge Pro Tem and a report and recommendation has not been issued, this motion will not be heard on the Law and Motion calendar. Although the parties have apparently stipulated to "bypassing" having this motion first heard by a Discovery Judge Pro Tem, neither the parties, nor a Judge Pro Tem, have the authority to do this. The motion will be heard on the Discovery calendar by a Judge Pro Tem on July 20, 2015. If the parties choose not to stipulate, then the motion will be subsequently heard on the Law and Motion calendar. (See Local Rule 10.0(B), "If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Temporary Judge will hold a hearing and issue a report and recommended ruling to the Law and Motion Department, and the judge presiding in the Law and Motion Department, will, at a later date, hold a hearing on the motion and decide it.") =302/EG

rulings.Case Title: LAMIS MALOUF ET AL VS. 24 HOUR FITNESS ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Leave To Amend Complaint
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, July 14, 2015, Line 5, PLAINTIFF LAMIS MALOUF's Motion For Leave To Amend Complaint The motion to amend is granted. Plaintiff's reply declaration meets the requirements of CRC 3.1324. There is a liberal policy in favor of amendment. There is no trial date and little prejudice to defendant in allowing amendment. Defendant fails to show that the proposed amendments are defective as a matter of law; defendant raises factual issues. See Cal. Casualty Gen. Ins. Co. v. Sup. Crt. (1983) 173 Cal.App.3d 274, 280 (disapproved on other grounds in Kransco v. American Empire Surplus Lines Ins. Co. (2000) 23 Cal.4th 390, 407). rulings.Case Title: WARREN LEGARIE VS. MAHMOUD ASSAF ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO COMPEL FURTHER RESPONSE TO REQUEST, DEMAND INSPECTION, PRODUCTION OF DOCUMENTS
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday July 14, 2015, Line 4, DEFENDANT TALAB QARAJAH'S MOTION TO COMPEL FURTHER RESPONSE TO REQUEST, DEMAND INSPECTION, PRODUCTION OF DOCUMENTS (part 1 of 2). Pro Tem Judge Katherine Gallo, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Motion Granted. All general objections are stricken. The Discovery Act does not authorize general objections. Instead the Discovery Act requires the party to respond in writing to each individual request for production of document. See C.C.P. §2031.210-240. All objections on the grounds of vague and unintelligible, overbroad and unreasonably burdensome are overruled. The court finds that plaintiff did not make a make a good faith effort in obtaining documents responsive to the request. Regency Health Services, Inc. v. Superior Court (1998) 64 CA4th 1496 This means that the obligation is well beyond providing the same response to 118 requests. See Sinaiko Healthcare Consulting, Inc. v. Pacific Healthcare Consultants (2007) 148 CA4th 390. When counsel for a party objects to production of documents under C.C.P. §2031.240(b), counsel implies that the documents in question exist and have been reviewed. Objection made to requests for production of document that do not exist or not in the attorney or party's possession violate an attorney's ethical duty under Bus & PC §6068(d) to act truthfully and, therefore, constitutes bad faith. See Bihun v. AT&T Info. Sys (1993) 13 CA4th 976, 991 (reversed on other grounds) and CEB §8:10. In providing the same response to 118 requests it is an indication that plaintiff did not review the responsive documents prior to serving the response. (continued on part 2) =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: WARREN LEGARIE VS. MAHMOUD ASSAF ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendant Talab Qarajah'S Motion To Compel Further Response To Request, Demand Inspection, Production Of Documents (Added For Posting Of Tentative Ruling)
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday July 14, 2015, Line 4, Defendant Talab Qarajah's Motion To Compel Further Response To Request, Demand Inspection, Production Of Documents (Added For Posting Of Tentative Ruling) (part 2 of 2). The court further finds that the privilege log plaintiff provided is not in compliance with C.C.P. §2031.240. The purpose of the privilege log in discovery proceedings is to provide specific factual description of documents in aid of substantiating a claim of privilege in connection with a request for document production, and is intended to permit a judicial evaluation of the claim of privilege. Best Products, Inc. v. Superior Court (2004) 119 CA4th 1181. A privilege log should state (a) the nature of the document (e.g., letter, memorandum, (b) date, (c) author, (d) recipients, (e) the sequential number (or document control umber, if any), and (f) the privilege claimed. See California Civil Discovery Practice (CEB 4th Ed. 2014) §3.192 citing Wells Fargo Bank v. Superior Court (2000) 22 C4th 201 and §33.201 for a sample of a privilege log. Plaintiff Warren Legarie to serve a further verified response and produce documents by July 31, 2015. Response to be in compliance with C.C.P. §2031.210-240. If plaintiff has already produced documents in compliance with the document request then the response shall so state. Defendant's request for sanctions in the amount of $1760.00 against Plaintiff Warren Legarie is granted. Check to be made payable to Talab Qarajah and his attorney of record Tour-Sarkissian Law offices and to be served no later than July 31, 2015 at the Sarkissian Law offices. Plaintiff's request for sanctions is denied. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to klgallo@discoveryreferee.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. (end of tenative ruling) =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: DANIEL DEMETRIS VS. COURTNEY SANCHEZ ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, July 14, 2015, Line 9, DEFENDANT MAJESTIC HALL LLC's DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT Defendant's demurrer is sustained without leave to amend. Absolute privilege statute bars civil liability for a tort claim based upon a privileged communication, with the exception of malicious prosecution, whose requirements include malice, lack of probable cause, and termination in the plaintiff's favor. [See Cal.Civ.Code § 47(b); Hagberg v. California Fed. Bank FSB (2004) 32 Cal. 4th 39, 81.] Here, Plaintiff's causes of action are based on Defendant's statements to the police, which are privileged and cannot be the basis of tort claims. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/eg
